mitre_attack: T1592.004 - Gather Victim Network Information: Network Topology
T1589.001 - Gather Victim Identity Information: Credentials
T1592.001 - Gather Victim Host Information: Hardware
cwe: CWE-209 - Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
CWE-497 - Exposure of Sensitive System Information to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
CWE-200 - Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

threat.title: Sensitive Information Disclosure via Stack Traces in Production Error Messages
threat.description: When applications expose detailed error messages and stack traces in production environments, attackers gain valuable reconnaissance information including internal file paths, database schemas, framework versions, third-party dependencies, environment variables, and application logic. This information significantly lowers the barrier for follow-up attacks such as SQL injection, remote code execution, and privilege escalation.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker sends malformed requests to various API endpoints (invalid JSON, missing parameters, SQL injection attempts)
2. Application responds with 500 Internal Server Error containing full stack trace
3. Stack trace reveals:
   - Internal file paths (/home/app/src/controllers/UserController.java)
   - Database connection strings with partial credentials
   - Framework versions (Spring Boot 3.1.0, Express 4.18.2)
   - ORM library versions (Hibernate 6.2.0, Sequelize 6.35.0)
   - Third-party dependency names and versions
   - Environment-specific configuration (database host, cache servers)
4. Attacker uses disclosed framework versions to search CVE databases
5. Identifies known vulnerability in disclosed dependency (e.g., CVE-2023-20883 in Spring Boot)
6. Exploits the specific vulnerability with high confidence, bypassing authentication or executing arbitrary code
prerequisites: Application runs in production mode with debug/verbose error handling enabled
No global error handler configured to sanitize responses
Stack traces not filtered or redacted before sending to clients
Error logging includes sensitive information without masking

cve_references.description: Spring Boot applications using Spring Expression Language (SpEL) in error messages could allow attackers to execute arbitrary code when detailed error messages containing user input are exposed
Apache Log4j stack trace deserialization vulnerability - information disclosure via error logging led to remote code execution when stack traces contained serialized objects
jQuery prototype pollution disclosed through detailed error messages in Express.js applications, enabling object injection attacks

real_world_breaches.incident: British Airways Data Breach - Information Gathering Phase
real_world_breaches.impact: 380,000 payment card details stolen; £20 million GDPR fine. Attack reconnaissance included error message analysis revealing vulnerable JavaScript library versions
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Attackers leveraged verbose error messages during initial reconnaissance to identify vulnerable Modernizr JavaScript library, enabling card skimming attack

real_world_breaches.incident: OWASP API Security Top 10 - API8:2023 Security Misconfiguration
real_world_breaches.impact: Stack traces in production named as #3 most common API security misconfiguration, affecting 31% of surveyed APIs
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Default error handling in development mode inadvertently deployed to production

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Stack trace disclosure revealing database schema and AWS RDS endpoint, leading to successful SQL injection attack
Express.js application exposing MongoDB connection string with credentials in error message

security_research.title: OWASP Testing Guide - Error Handling (WSTG-ERRH-01)
security_research.key_finding: Error messages should never reveal sensitive information about the application's internal structure, database schema, or system configuration

security_research.title: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through an Error Message
security_research.key_finding: Error messages containing system data, stack traces, database dumps, or file paths are consistently exploited in multi-stage attacks

mitigations.title: Spring Boot Custom Error Handling with ControllerAdvice
mitigations.description: Implement a global exception handler using @ControllerAdvice and @ExceptionHandler to intercept all exceptions, log detailed errors internally, and return sanitized generic error messages to clients.
nist_csf: PR.PT-1 - Audit/log records are determined and documented
DE.AE-3 - Event data are collected and correlated
validation_steps: Verify client receives generic error messages without stack traces
Confirm detailed errors are logged server-side with correlation IDs
Test that application.yml has 'include-stacktrace: never' in production profile
Use OWASP ZAP or Burp Suite to scan for information disclosure

mitigations.title: Express.js Custom Error Middleware with Helmet Security Headers
mitigations.description: Implement centralized error handling middleware as the last middleware in the Express.js chain. Use Helmet.js to set security headers and ensure NODE_ENV=production to suppress stack traces.
validation_steps: Verify NODE_ENV=production is set in environment
Test error endpoints and confirm no stack traces in response
Validate error logs contain full details with correlation IDs
Use 'npm audit' to check for vulnerabilities in helmet and express

mitigations.title: Production-Mode Configuration and Environment Validation
mitigations.description: Enforce production-mode settings through environment variables, configuration management, and deployment pipeline validation to prevent accidental exposure of debug information.

code_example.code:
# ============================================
# Docker Production Configuration
# ============================================
# Dockerfile.production
FROM node:20-alpine AS base

# Set production environment BEFORE installing dependencies
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NPM_CONFIG_LOGLEVEL=error

WORKDIR /app

# Install production dependencies only
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --only=production && npm cache clean --force

COPY . .

# Run as non-root user
RUN addgroup -g 1001 -S nodejs && \
    adduser -S nodejs -u 1001 && \
    chown -R nodejs:nodejs /app

USER nodejs

EXPOSE 3000

# Health check
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=40s \
  CMD node healthcheck.js || exit 1

CMD ["node", "server.js"]

# ============================================
# Kubernetes Deployment (deployment.yaml)
# ============================================
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: api-service
  namespace: production
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: api-service
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: api-service
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: api
        image: myregistry.azurecr.io/api:1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 3000
        env:
        # Critical: Enforce production mode
        - name: NODE_ENV
          value: "production"
        - name: LOG_LEVEL
          value: "warn"
        # Load secrets from Azure Key Vault via CSI driver
        - name: DATABASE_URL
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: database-secrets
              key: connection-string
        # Resource limits
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "1000m"
            memory: "512Mi"
          requests:
            cpu: "500m"
            memory: "256Mi"
        # Liveness and readiness probes
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 3000
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /ready
            port: 3000
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 5
        # Security context
        securityContext:
          runAsNonRoot: true
          runAsUser: 1001
          allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
          readOnlyRootFilesystem: true

---
# ============================================
# Azure App Service Configuration
# ============================================
# azure-pipelines.yml (deployment stage)
- task: AzureWebApp@1
  inputs:
    azureSubscription: 'Production-ServiceConnection'
    appName: 'my-api-prod'
    package: '$(Pipeline.Workspace)/drop/app.zip'
    appSettings: |
      [
        {
          "name": "NODE_ENV",
          "value": "production",
          "slotSetting": false
        },
        {
          "name": "WEBSITE_NODE_DEFAULT_VERSION",
          "value": "20.x",
          "slotSetting": false
        },
        {
          "name": "SCM_DO_BUILD_DURING_DEPLOYMENT",
          "value": "false",
          "slotSetting": false
        }
      ]

# ============================================
# Spring Boot Production Configuration
# ============================================
# application-prod.yml
server:
  port: 8080
  error:
    include-message: never
    include-binding-errors: never
    include-stacktrace: never
    include-exception: false
    whitelabel:
      enabled: false

spring:
  profiles:
    active: prod
  jpa:
    show-sql: false
    properties:
      hibernate:
        format_sql: false
  mvc:
    throw-exception-if-no-handler-found: true
  web:
    resources:
      add-mappings: false

logging:
  level:
    root: WARN
    com.example: INFO
    org.springframework: WARN
    org.hibernate: ERROR
  pattern:
    console: "%d{yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss} [%thread] %-5level %logger{36} - %msg%n"
  file:
    name: /var/log/app/application.log
    max-size: 10MB
    max-history: 30

management:
  endpoints:
    web:
      exposure:
        include: health,info,metrics
  endpoint:
    health:
      show-details: when-authorized

# ============================================
# CI/CD Validation Script
# ============================================
# scripts/validate-production-config.sh
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "Validating production configuration..."

# Check NODE_ENV
if [ "$NODE_ENV" != "production" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: NODE_ENV must be 'production', got '$NODE_ENV'"
    exit 1
fi

# Check for development dependencies in package.json
if grep -q '"devDependencies"' package-lock.json 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "WARNING: devDependencies found in production build"
fi

# Check for debug configurations
if grep -rq "console.log\|debugger" src/ 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "WARNING: Debug statements found in source code"
fi

# Verify Helmet is installed
if ! grep -q '"helmet"' package.json; then
    echo "ERROR: Helmet.js not found in dependencies"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Production configuration validation passed"

iso27001_controls: A.12.1.4 - Separation of development, testing, and operational environments
nist_csf: PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle to manage systems is implemented
PR.PT-3 - The principle of least functionality is incorporated
validation_steps: Run validation script in CI/CD pipeline before deployment
Verify environment variables are set correctly in deployment manifest
Test production deployment and confirm error responses are sanitized
Review application logs to ensure detailed errors are captured
Use Azure Policy or OPA to enforce production configuration standards
Perform quarterly security reviews of error handling configuration

indicators: High volume of 500 Internal Server Error responses from specific endpoints
Repeated requests with malformed payloads designed to trigger exceptions
Error responses containing file paths (e.g., /home/, /usr/, C:\)
Error messages revealing database connection strings or schema details
Unusual user-agent strings associated with vulnerability scanners (Nikto, SQLMap, OWASP ZAP)
Sequential requests testing different error conditions (reconnaissance pattern)

log_queries.query:
// Detect high volume of 500 errors with potential stack trace exposure
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where ScStatus == 500
| extend ResponseSize = todouble(ScBytes)
| where ResponseSize > 2000 // Stack traces are typically large
| summarize ErrorCount = count(), 
            UniqueIPs = dcount(CIp),
            AvgResponseSize = avg(ResponseSize)
    by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m), CsHost, CsUriStem
| where ErrorCount > 10 or UniqueIPs > 5
| project TimeGenerated, CsHost, CsUriStem, ErrorCount, UniqueIPs, AvgResponseSize
| order by ErrorCount desc

sequence by source.ip with maxspan=5m
  [http where http.response.status_code == 500]
  [http where http.response.status_code == 500]
  [http where http.response.status_code == 500]
until [http where http.response.status_code == 200]

index=web_logs status=500 
| eval response_length=len(response_body)
| where response_length > 2000
| regex response_body="(at\s+[a-zA-Z]+\.(java|js):\d+|Exception|Error:|Stack\s+trace)"
| stats count as error_count, 
        dc(client_ip) as unique_ips,
        values(endpoint) as affected_endpoints,
        avg(response_length) as avg_size
    by _time span=5m
| where error_count > 5
| sort -error_count

fields @timestamp, @message, request.url, response.statusCode, response.body
| filter response.statusCode = 500
| filter strlen(response.body) > 2000
| stats count() as errorCount by bin(5m) as interval, request.url
| filter errorCount > 10
| sort errorCount desc

tools.name: Burp Suite
tools.usage: Scan for information disclosure issues via Burp Scanner with 'Error Messages' checks enabled

compliance: ISO27001 (A.12.4.1 - Event Logging, A.18.2.3 - Technical Compliance)
NIST 800-53 (AU-3 - Content of Audit Records, SI-11 - Error Handling)
